class.name: CrowdControl
class.brief_description: A client for interacting with the CrowdControl API natively in Blazium.
class.description:
The [CrowdControl] singleton allows you to connect your Blazium game to the CrowdControl interactivity service. Using this module, streamers can allow their viewers to trigger in-game effects either instantly or over a timed duration.
[CrowdControl] uses WebSockets for real-time interaction and HTTP polling as a fallback or auxiliary authentication context.
You must register your application on the CrowdControl Developer Portal to obtain an App ID and Secret.

method.name: close
method.description: Closes the active CrowdControl WebSocket connection and stops polling.

method.name: connect_to_crowdcontrol
method.description: Initiates the WebSocket connection to the CrowdControl PubSub servers. By default, it uses the production wss url. Emits [signal connection_established] on success.

method.name: get_auth_token
method.description: Returns the current authentication token obtained after a successful login. It can be cached and restored later using [method set_auth_token].

method.name: get_authentication_url
method.description: Returns the URL that the player must visit to authorize the game with CrowdControl. Usually emitted via [signal authentication_url_ready].

method.name: get_cc_uid
method.description: Returns the CrowdControl unique identifier for the authenticated user.

method.name: get_connection_id
method.description: Returns the active WebSocket connection ID.

method.name: get_game_session_id
method.description: Returns the ID of the currently active game session, or an empty string if no session is active.

method.name: get_refresh_token
method.description: Returns the refresh token, if any, that can be used to seamlessly renew an expired authentication session via [method refresh_token].

method.name: get_username
method.description: Returns the display name of the authenticated user.

method.name: is_authenticated
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if a valid authentication token is currently held.

method.name: is_websocket_connected
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the underlying WebSocket is currently connected to the PubSub servers.

method.name: poll
method.description: Polls the underlying WebSocket connections and message queues. You must call this every frame (e.g., in [method Node._process]) to ensure CrowdControl receives and processes messages.

method.name: refresh_token
method.description: Attempts to seamlessly renew the current authentication session using a previously cached refresh token, or the provided [param refresh_token].

method.name: report_effects
method.description: Informs the CrowdControl service that the specified effects are now available, unavailable, visible, or hidden based on their [param status]. Useful for dynamic game states (e.g., hiding a "Spawn Enemy" effect while the player is in a safe-zone).

method.name: request_authentication_http
method.description: Requests an authentication session through traditional HTTP polling. Use this as an alternative to [method request_authentication_websocket]. Wait for the [signal authentication_url_ready] signal for the authorization link.

method.name: request_authentication_websocket
method.description:
Requests an authentication session through the active WebSocket. Wait for the [signal authentication_url_ready] signal, which provides a link you must present to the player to authorize the game.
[codeblocks] [gdscript] CrowdControl.request_authentication_websocket() var url = await CrowdControl.authentication_url_ready print("Please authorize: ", url) await CrowdControl.authenticated print("Authenticated!") [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

method.name: request_interact_link
method.description: Requests a public interactivity link that viewers can visit to interact with the game. Connect to [signal interact_link_received] to get the generated link.

method.name: respond_to_effect_instant
method.description:
Responds to an immediate effect request triggered by [signal effect_requested]. Send [constant STATUS_SUCCESS] if the effect fired, or [constant STATUS_FAILURE] if it failed.
[codeblocks] [gdscript] func _on_effect_requested(data): var req_id = data["reqId"] var code = data["code"] if code == "givecoins": # give coins CrowdControl.respond_to_effect_instant(req_id, CrowdControl.STATUS_SUCCESS, "Coins given!") [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

method.name: respond_to_effect_timed
method.description:
Responds to a timed effect request (an effect that lasts for [param time_remaining] milliseconds). Start the effect with [constant STATUS_TIMED_BEGIN], and when the time expires, call this again with [constant STATUS_TIMED_END].
[codeblocks] [gdscript] # Start the effect CrowdControl.respond_to_effect_timed(req_id, CrowdControl.STATUS_TIMED_BEGIN, 30000, "Invert Controls started.")
# Later, when the effect wears off CrowdControl.respond_to_effect_timed(req_id, CrowdControl.STATUS_TIMED_END, 0, "Controls restored.") [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

method.name: set_auth_token
method.description: Restores a previously saved authentication session. This allows your game to remember the user's login without requiring them to authorize in their browser again.

method.name: set_credentials
method.description: Configures the CrowdControl client with your Developer app ID and Secret. Must be called before connecting or authenticating.

method.name: set_http_base_url
method.description: Overrides the default REST HTTP base URL (which defaults to [code]https://openapi.crowdcontrol.live[/code]).

method.name: start_game_session
method.description: Notifies the server that a new game session has begun, bound to the specified [param game_pack_id] (which dictates the available effects).

method.name: stop_game_session
method.description: Stops the currently active game session.

signal.name: authenticated
signal.description: Emitted when the authentication flow successfully finishes and the user is logged in. Provides the access [param token], unique [param cc_uid], and display [param username].

signal.name: authentication_url_ready
signal.description: Emitted after requesting authentication. The game must display or open this [param url] for the player to log in via their browser.

signal.name: connection_closed
signal.description: Emitted when the persistent WebSocket connection closes. Includes the status [param code] and string [param reason].

signal.name: connection_error
signal.description: Emitted when the WebSocket connection to the CrowdControl PubSub servers encounters an error.

signal.name: connection_established
signal.description: Emitted when [method connect_to_crowdcontrol] successfully establishes a WebSocket connection.

signal.name: effect_requested
signal.description: Emitted when a viewer triggers an effect. The [param effect_data] dictionary contains properties like [code]reqId[/code], [code]code[/code], and [code]parameters[/code]. Check the [code]code[/code] to determine which effect to apply, and respond using [method respond_to_effect_instant] or [method respond_to_effect_timed].

signal.name: game_session_started
signal.description: Emitted when a game session has been successfully created. Effects can now be requested by viewers.

signal.name: game_session_stopped
signal.description: Emitted when a game session ends.

signal.name: interact_link_received
signal.description: Emitted after [method request_interact_link] finishes with the [param link] players can give viewers to interact with the current game state via the browser.

signal.name: rpc_error
signal.description: Emitted when an RPC call receives an error payload.
